mnemonics.title: Critical Section Requirements
mnemonics.content: REMEMBER THE THREE MPS: MUTUAL EXCLUSION (only one), PROGRESS (no unnecessary delay), BOUNDED WAITING (no starvation). THINK 'MPB' FOR SHORT.

mnemonics.title: Peterson's Algorithm Variables
mnemonics.content: FLAGS TELL US 'I WANT IN', TURN SAYS 'WHO GOES NOW'. THINK 'FLAG = INTEREST, TURN = DECIDER'.

mnemonics.title: CS Problem Structure
mnemonics.content: ENTER -> CRITICAL -> EXIT -> REMAINDER. THINK 'E-C-E-R' FOR SHORT.

acronyms.usage: The portion of code accessing shared resources requiring mutual exclusion
Ensuring only one process in critical section at a time
Hardware atomic instruction for implementing locks
Code that does not access shared resources
Unpredictable outcome due to concurrent access timing

visualTips.visualization: DRAW TWO PROCESS boxes (P0, P1). BETWEEN THEM, SHOW A 'TURN' arrow AND TWO 'FLAG' switches. WHEN P0 wants in, flip flag[0]=ON. IF flag[1]=OFF, P0 enters; IF flag[1]=ON, wait for turn=0.
DRAW A LOCK box WITH 'value=0' (unlocked). TEST-AND-SET READS value, SETS value=1, RETURNS OLD VALUE. THINK OF IT AS: 'GRAB AND MARK SIMULTANEOUSLY'.
DRAW A NUMBERED QUEUE (1,2,3...). BOUNDED WAITING MEANS AFTER REQUEST #3, AT MOST N-1 OTHERS CAN ENTER BEFORE #3 GETS IN.

visualTips.visualize: DRAW AN EMPTY ROOM (critical section). IF NO ONE INSIDE AND SOMEONE WANTS IN, DRAW AN ARROW DIRECTLY INTO THE ROOM - NO WAITING IN BETWEEN.

analogies.analogy: THINK OF A SINGLE-occupancy bathroom in an office. Only one person can use it at a time. The bathroom is the critical section, and the lock on the door enforces mutual exclusion.
TWO PEOPLE TRYING TO SIGN THE SAME DOCUMENT WITH DIFFERENT PENS AT THE SAME TIME. The final signature depends on who finishes last - unpredictable and potentially invalid.
TWO FRIENDS DECIDING WHO PLAYS A VIDEO GAME. ONE SAYS 'I WANT TO PLAY', THE OTHER SAYS 'OK, YOU GO'. IF BOTH WANT, THE ONE WHOSE TURN IT IS GOES FIRST.
A DOCTOR'S CLINIC WITH A NUMBERED TICKET SYSTEM. AFTER YOU TAKE A NUMBER, AT MOST 5 PEOPLE CAN BE SERVED BEFORE YOUR NUMBER IS CALLED.
STANDING AT A BUS STOP AND CONTINUOUSLY LOOKING DOWN THE ROAD TO SEE IF THE BUS IS COMING, EVEN THOUGH YOU COULD SIT AND WAIT TO BE CALLED.
